tasks.title: Extend the song model with tablature events
acceptance_criteria: A measure can carry timed six-string fret events without changing existing chord-only songs.
At least one existing sample song contains a representative playable TAB phrase.
done_definition: TypeScript accepts both chord-only and chord-plus-TAB measures.

epics.title: Render and play the guitar score in the browser

tasks.title: Add responsive six-line tablature below chord notation
acceptance_criteria: Chord symbols, barlines, section structure, and six TAB strings render in one continuous score system.
Fret numbers align to beats and strings.
Representative systems fit the available desktop and narrow pane without page-level horizontal overflow.
Section labels are not repeated when a nearby heading already establishes context.
The implementation follows the local design-rule-engine and relevant Next.js, shadcn, and Geist guidance.
done_definition: The selected sample visibly reads as a guitar score rather than a chord-only lead sheet.

tasks.title: Add compact score playback controls and cursor
acceptance_criteria: A single primary play/pause control starts and pauses a deterministic visual cursor.
Restart returns playback to the beginning.
Tempo is visible and the cursor does not require Guitar Pro or an uploaded proprietary file.
Controls expose accessible names and status.
done_definition: A user can follow the rendered TAB with a moving position indicator.

tasks.title: Run deterministic and browser-backed checks
acceptance_criteria: Lint, typecheck, build, and relevant tests pass.
Before and after browser evidence is captured.
Desktop and narrow viewport verification checks layout width, representative score rows, redundant labels, and interactive controls.
Rendered markup and served styles visibly produce TAB lines and fret-number placement.
done_definition: Verification and both design/code reviews are approved with no major findings.

assumptions: This iteration renders the repository's structured song data and does not import proprietary Guitar Pro files.
Visual cursor playback is sufficient for the first iteration; audio synthesis and editing are future work.
The existing warm restrained editorial utility profile remains appropriate and the screen is not brand-critical.
risks: Dense notation can become illegible on narrow screens if measure density is not reduced responsively.
Playback timing can drift if the cursor is modeled only with repeated timers instead of elapsed time.
A visual TAB sample may imply full Guitar Pro compatibility unless the interface labels the current capability clearly.
